name: MIDDLE EAR / TYMPANIC CAVITY
definition: air-filled cavity within petrous portion of temporal bone containing ossicular chain carotid wall mastoid wall including from lateral to medial small triangular fragile 2-layered membrane in upper region loosely attached to larger robust tightly attached region consisting of 3 layers (skin + fibrous tissue + rounded promontory

demographics.Location: above malleolar folds; forms superior ⅛ of drum circumference

dominant_finding: may be faintly discerned on CT
distribution: above malleolar folds; forms superior ⅛ of drum circumference
discriminating_features: may be faintly discerned on CT
wall prominence inferior and anterior to (1)

general: may be faintly discerned on CT
wall prominence inferior and anterior to (1)
recess on posterior wall lateral to (6)
situated between sinus tympani + facial nerve recess
